analysis.summary: Create a regularly maintainable statewide list that connects transit-priority projects to location needs, applicable plans, and eligible funding.

expectedOutputs.text: A versioned statewide priority-project list with funding eligibility and plan crosswalks

dependencies.text: Stable project and location identifiers plus an update and approval process

openQuestions: What determines inclusion and priority?
What is the refresh and conflict-resolution process?

analysis.summary: Establish a statewide mobility-management role that coordinates trips and shared planning, scheduling, payment, and asset technologies across grantees.

expectedOutputs.text: A mobility-management operating model and portfolio of shared coordination tools

dependencies.text: Governance, data stewardship, procurement, cost allocation, and participant onboarding

openQuestions: Which formal action establishes the role?
Which services are shared, optional, or required?

analysis.summary: Offer eligible Section 5307 jurisdictions the option to use Caltrans as direct recipient and coordinate resulting federal changes.

expectedOutputs.text: Jurisdiction notice, election workflow, and federal recipient-change process

dependencies.text: Eligible-jurisdiction register, recipient-readiness controls, and federal compliance procedures

openQuestions: How is “more rapid” disbursement measured?
How do compliance duties and reversibility work after an election?

analysis.summary: Prepare future State Transit Plan iterations to use service-led planning aligned with the State Rail Plan.

expectedOutputs.text: A documented service-led planning method and rail-plan alignment crosswalk

dependencies.text: A shared definition of service-led planning and measurable alignment

openQuestions: What steps satisfy the directive before the next plan is published?
How will alignment be measured and approved?

analysis.summary: Start and complete a coordinated set of design, specification, policy, and permitting materials for transit infrastructure on the State Highway System.

expectedOutputs.text: Published design guidance, standard plans and specifications, policies, procedures, and updated manuals

dependencies.text: Named document owners, adoption authorities, accessibility requirements, and alignment with traffic-control standards

openQuestions: Which exact documents are in scope?
What review and adoption process applies to each artifact?

analysis.summary: Explain to the transportation agency how recent CEQA changes can be applied to expedite transit projects after consultation with local partners.

expectedOutputs.text: A report connecting recent CEQA changes to eligible transit-project pathways

dependencies.text: Legal and project-category crosswalk, local consultation record, and a baseline for expedited delivery

openQuestions: Will the report be public?
Which project categories and measures define expedited delivery?

analysis.summary: Create a statewide team that can add technical and delivery capacity for state-funded active-transportation projects.

expectedOutputs.text: A staffed support team with an intake, triage, assistance, and delivery-capacity model

dependencies.text: Charter, staffing and budget, project inventory, service levels, and outcome measures

openQuestions: Where will the team sit organizationally?
How are projects prioritized and how long does support last?

analysis.summary: Create funding-program dashboards covering applications, awards, contracting, reimbursement, and other performance measures to support transparency and equitable access.

expectedOutputs.text: Public dashboards and a documented metric/data model spanning available transit and rail funding programs

dependencies.text: Canonical program, cycle, application, award, contract, and invoice timestamps with public-data rules

openQuestions: Which programs and records are in scope?
What update interval and system of record support the term “real time”?

analysis.summary: Explore lawful ways to help regions shift federal highway formula funds to transit, including possible state-share matching.

expectedOutputs.text: An opportunity and eligibility analysis for reprogramming and state-match support

dependencies.text: Program-by-program legal eligibility, state-match availability, and a regional project pipeline

openQuestions: What artifact completes the instruction to explore?
Which programs, match sources, and decision authorities are included?

analysis.summary: Take steps to automate Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting for rural and small agencies.

expectedOutputs.text: A field-level reporting crosswalk, automation boundary, provenance model, and implementation roadmap

dependencies.text: Authoritative reporting definitions, source-system evidence, validation, audit trail, and submitter certification

openQuestions: How are rural, small, and automate defined?
Which fields can be derived, which can only be assisted, and who remains the legal submitter?

analysis.summary: Reduce fund-distribution timelines where feasible and examine additional flexibility for uses and federal pre-award authority.

expectedOutputs.text: Program-specific timeline-reduction and fund-flexibility measures

dependencies.text: Baseline and target timelines plus legal authority for each administrative or policy change

openQuestions: Which programs and process stages are covered?
Which changes are administrative, statutory, or federal?

analysis.summary: Begin pursuing federal environmental-review assignment for transit and commuter-rail projects, subject to federal law.

expectedOutputs.text: An assignment pathway with legal authority, agreements, staffing, and quality controls

dependencies.text: Federal eligibility, state authority, liability terms, and an assignment agreement

openQuestions: Which assignment mechanism and project classes are contemplated?
What state legislation or agreement would be required?

analysis.summary: Offer feasible planning, engineering, implementation, and cooperative-agreement assistance for BRT and bus-only infrastructure on state highways.

expectedOutputs.text: A technical-assistance offer, intake route, and cooperative-agreement pathway

dependencies.text: Standard assistance scope, project intake, agreements, and design/permitting guidance

openQuestions: Is assistance request-driven or tied to the priority-project list?
What phases, service levels, and cost-sharing rules apply?

analysis.summary: Create an implementation plan for a centralized digital real-property asset management system, fully digitize Caltrans real-estate holdings, and inventory buildings and their state of repair in coordination with DGS.

expectedOutputs.text: A delivery model linking the implementation plan, completed holdings digitization, and building repair-state inventory

dependencies.text: Canonical property identifiers, geospatial and condition standards, system ownership, and digitization rules

openQuestions: What completion criteria and quality controls define “fully digitize”?
Which holdings, buildings, and repair-state categories are in scope?

analysis.summary: Identify airspace-policy changes that could allow existing operator-controlled storage sites to support zero-emission vehicle storage and energy infrastructure.

expectedOutputs.text: A documented set of exception-policy updates and eligible-site conditions

dependencies.text: Current policy, parcel and airspace inventory, safety and structural review, utilities, and operator agreements

openQuestions: Does identify include adoption and implementation?
How are operator control, existing storage, and eligible sites defined?

analysis.summary: Develop lawful guidance for directing excess toll revenue toward unmet transit operating and capital needs, including managed-lane corridors.

expectedOutputs.text: Guidance defining eligible needs, excess revenue, corridor relationship, and prioritization

dependencies.text: Toll-facility and revenue inventory plus state and federal legal restrictions

openQuestions: Is the guidance binding or advisory?
How are excess revenue, unmet need, and corridor benefit calculated?

analysis.summary: Start a California traffic-control-manual update for transit infrastructure and seek any required federal approvals.

expectedOutputs.text: A scoped MUTCD update, stakeholder process, draft, approvals, and adoption path

dependencies.text: Manual change control, federal conformity, stakeholder review, and alignment with design standards

openQuestions: Which chapters and devices are in scope?
What draft, approval, and adoption milestones apply?

analysis.summary: Develop criteria that prioritize both funding and award processing for transit projects in Prohousing-designated jurisdictions.

expectedOutputs.text: Program criteria and processing rules tied to an authoritative Prohousing designation record

dependencies.text: Designation data, effective dates, cross-jurisdiction rules, and program scoring authority

openQuestions: Which programs and weighting or queue mechanics are covered?
How are projects spanning jurisdictions and designation changes handled?

analysis.summary: Identify and implement opportunities to use CHSRA’s FRA NEPA Delegation for State Rail Plan projects, working with the Caltrans Division of Rail and, where possible, undertaking programmatic environmental review.

expectedOutputs.text: An eligibility and delivery pathway for candidate State Rail Plan projects

dependencies.text: Canonical rail-project IDs, delegation scope, sponsor acceptance and reimbursement, staffing, and candidate criteria

openQuestions: Which projects are eligible and who funds the review?
How are candidates prioritized against available review capacity?

analysis.summary: Update state program guidelines to incentivize transit-priority and first- and last-mile delivery, and identify federal programs that support those efforts.

expectedOutputs.text: Updated state program guidelines with defined incentives
A documented identification of applicable federal funding programs

dependencies.text: Program-cycle calendars, legal authority, shared definitions and measures, and federal eligibility rules

openQuestions: Which guideline cycles and owners apply?
How are transit priority, connectivity, and incentive magnitude defined?

analysis.summary: Publish transit and passenger-rail data-availability guidelines through Cal-ITP and implement a coordinated grant CRM through CIMP in partnership with Caltrans IT.

expectedOutputs.text: Transit and passenger-rail data availability guidelines with standards and governance
A grant CRM covering availability, applications, management, and information campaigns

dependencies.text: Standards inventory, version governance, canonical grant schema, product ownership, integration, migration, privacy, accessibility, and language controls

openQuestions: Which data domains and standards are mandatory or advisory?
Is the CRM the system of record behind the funding dashboards, and what does “single source” mean operationally?
